note: Acidspar + H2SO4 → HF. Every downstream fluorine chain starts here.
F2 / HF feed the fluorination of tungsten metal to WF6.
HF/F2 feedstock for NF3 synthesis.
HF → PF5 → LiPF6 via reaction with LiF.
Yellowcake is converted to UF6 using HF/F2.
Wet-etch and clean chemistries sit in the same fab BOM as resists; fluorinated solvents also feed resist formulation.
Via HF chain.
Via HF chain.
Via HF chain into battery electrolyte.
Via HF chain into nuclear fuel conversion.
Both gate lithography throughput — neon for DUV excimer lasers, resist for the patterning itself.
Leading-edge logic dies printed with EUV resist are packaged on ABF substrates.
Both sit upstream of every wafer — quartz via crucibles, resist via lithography.
Both are narrow geographic-concentration chokepoints upstream of leading-edge silicon and packaging.
Shared silica-and-photonics adjacency; preforms are silica doped with Ge.
Data-centre build-out depends on both AI-accelerator substrates (ABF) and optical fibre (GeCl4-doped preforms).
Both consumed in-line at the same fab, sharing feedstock risk (HF/fluorspar) and Korean/Japanese producer concentration.
Both are HF-downstream chemistries — nuclear fuel conversion and battery electrolyte share the same fluorine feed stack.
Lithium refining upstream of LiPF6 runs on SX extractants; phosphorus chain is shared.
Both are invisible upstream reagent/mineral chokepoints for the critical-minerals refining stack.
